document.source_title: IPL 2026 playoffs qualification: Scenarios for all 8 teams in contention explained

claims.claim_text: Rajat Patidar’s team can secure a playoff spot with a win against KKR.
claims.evidence_text: If Rajat Patidar’s team defeat KKR on Wednesday night, they will have one foot in the IPL play-offs.

claims.claim_text: RCB has the best net run rate among the teams in contention.
claims.evidence_text: With 14 points from seven wins in 11 games, RCB have the best net run rate among the teams in contention.

claims.claim_text: Punjab Kings need to win their next matches to qualify.
claims.evidence_text: Two wins in their final three games against Mumbai Indians, Bengaluru and Sunrisers Hyderabad should be enough.

claims.claim_text: Gujarat Titans are on course to secure a top two qualification.
claims.evidence_text: With a bowling line up firing on all cylinders and a solid top three, the Shubman Gill-led side is on course to secure a top two qualification.

claims.claim_text: Chennai Super Kings need to win all their remaining games to qualify.
claims.evidence_text: Only 18 points will ensure qualification with teams in contention playing each other over the course of the next 10 days, leaving CSK to win all their remaining games.

claims.claim_text: Delhi Capitals have a slim chance of making the playoffs.
claims.evidence_text: Delhi’s hopes hang by a thread though the Axar Patel-led side has already started planning for the next season.

entities.name: Rajat Patidar’s team
entities.description: A team in contention for IPL playoffs.
entities.evidence_text: If Rajat Patidar’s team defeat KKR on Wednesday night, they will have one foot in the IPL play-offs.

entities.name: RCB
entities.description: Royal Challengers Bangalore, a team in contention for IPL playoffs.
entities.evidence_text: With 14 points from seven wins in 11 games, RCB have the best net run rate among the teams in contention.

entities.name: Punjab Kings
entities.description: A team in contention for IPL playoffs.
entities.evidence_text: After staying unbeaten in the first seven games of the season, Punjab Kings have lost four games in a row now to find themselves in fourth spot in the IPL standings.

entities.name: Gujarat Titans
entities.description: A team in contention for IPL playoffs.
entities.evidence_text: With a bowling line up firing on all cylinders and a solid top three, the Shubman Gill-led side is on course to secure a top two qualification.

entities.name: Chennai Super Kings
entities.description: A team in contention for IPL playoffs.
entities.evidence_text: After spending plenty of time near the bottom of the standings early in the season, Chennai Super Kings have risen at the right time.

entities.name: Delhi Capitals
entities.description: A team in contention for IPL playoffs.
entities.evidence_text: Delhi’s hopes hang by a thread though the Axar Patel-led side has already started planning for the next season.

events.event: Chennai Super Kings vs Lucknow Super Giants
events.evidence_text: They would back themselves to collect two points against Lucknow Super Giants on May 15 before taking on SRH and Gujarat Titans in their final two league games.

metrics.name: points
metrics.evidence_text: With 14 points from seven wins in 11 games, RCB have the best net run rate among the teams in contention.

metrics.name: points
metrics.evidence_text: For reference, only Mumbai Indians qualified with 16 points last season.

metrics.name: points
metrics.evidence_text: Only 18 points will ensure qualification with teams in contention playing each other over the course of the next 10 days.
